authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. construction safety regulator; this standard defines hazardous-condition refusal, frequent site inspections by competent persons, and site-safety duties that procurement cannot override.
used_for: separating procurement support from safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: Procurement Under Grants Policy Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FEMA procurement guidance is an authoritative public operating reference for competition, documentation, contract administration, and noncompliance handling when construction procurement is publicly funded.
used_for: when procurement support cannot bypass formal approval

authoritative_sources.title: After You Get a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: An official permitting authority source is required for this lane; NYC DOB provides authoritative public examples of permit-holder duties, plan posting, required inspections, and signoff responsibilities while local code details remain jurisdiction-specific.

authoritative_sources.title: DOB NOW: Inspections Guide: Construction Inspection Sign Off Prior to Occupancy Request
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official inspection guide is an authoritative public example of inspection signoff workflow, objection handling, and occupancy-sensitive supporting documents that materially affect release and closeout posture.

authoritative_sources.title: GSA 184 - Construction Progress Report
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official construction progress report is a federal operating reference for material delivery status, accepted material on site or in storage, shop-drawing submissions, schedule posture, and change-order visibility.

authoritative_sources.title: UFGS 01 33 00.05 20 - Construction Submittal Procedures
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / USACE / NAVFAC / AFCEC
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The Unified Facilities Guide Specifications are recognized federal construction standards that define preconstruction submittals, proposed subcontractor and product lists, submittal registers, test reports, certificates, and manufacturer documentation before work can safely proceed.

authoritative_sources.title: UFGS 01 78 00 - Closeout Submittals
authoritative_sources.publisher: Whole Building Design Guide / USACE / NAVFAC / AFCEC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This guide specification is a recognized public baseline for record drawings, warranties, O&M manuals, and turnover evidence needed before the lane can represent procurement work as complete.
used_for: preventing procurement completion from being misrepresented as project closeout

workflow_stages.name: Procurement intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, work-order scope, supplier context, permit posture, approved-submittal state, and tenant approval rules before treating supply as actionable.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Availability and replenishment assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare inventory, delivery, shortage, subcontractor sequence, and site-readiness evidence to determine whether materials can support the requested commercial or industrial project step.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Vendor action and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Package bounded supplier recommendations, expediting notes, and shortage escalations while preserving financial, substitution, inspection, and contract-administration boundaries.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Inspection, safety, and closeout dependency review
workflow_stages.description: Stop or escalate when safety controls, inspection hold points, permit status, or turnover artifacts materially change whether procurement support is safe or complete.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: work orders, buyout packages, purchase requests, approved supplier lists, and procurement logs
permits, permit postings, approved plans, inspection reports, correction notices, and inspection-signoff records
site-readiness checklists, site logs, safety observations, and hold-point records
submittals, approved alternates, RFIs, change orders, product data, and shop drawing status
supplier commitments, subcontractor documents, lead-time trackers, and expediting notes
inventory counts, serial or lot evidence, delivery receipts, and laydown-yard records
record drawings, O&M manuals, warranty records, turnover checklists, and closeout packages

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for construction procurement readiness
summarize supply, shortage, vendor, work-order, permit, inspection, and closeout dependencies with explicit evidence references
prepare bounded procurement recommendations and escalation packages without implying final approval authority
escalate: substitution, alternate, or means-and-methods decisions requiring design, engineer-of-record, or licensed-trade judgment
permit issuance, inspection approval, safety posture changes, or closeout signoff questions requiring formal authority
spend approval, contract commitment, approved-supplier override, or conflicting public-versus-tenant policy decisions
refuse: bypass spend, approved-supplier, permit, inspection, or safety controls to preserve schedule
represent unverified inventory or delivery evidence as ready for installation
treat procurement tracking as authority to approve code-sensitive substitutions, trade signoff, or permit readiness

domain_failure_modes: Materials are treated as available even though permit, inspection, or site-readiness records are incomplete.
Supplier or shortage pressure causes the lane to bypass spend approval, approved-supplier controls, or documented submittal states.
A substitution request with code or trade implications is handled as routine procurement instead of escalated to licensed authority.
Inventory is reported as ready without authoritative serial, lot, laydown, or delivery evidence.
Procurement completion is confused with inspection completion, closeout completion, or occupancy readiness.
Safety hold points or inspection comments are ignored because the lane focuses only on material timing.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Required supply is unavailable or short
Vendor or substitution decision exceeds documented authority
Traceability or inventory evidence is incomplete

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit classes, named inspections, staging rules, and occupancy terminology
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public permitting workflow is authoritative only at the jurisdiction level. The pack therefore requires tenant or jurisdiction retrieval for exact permit classes, local hold points, material-staging restrictions, and occupancy or closeout terms instead of guessing from a single city baseline.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Whether a given project is governed by public-funding procurement controls or private-project commercial controls
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: FEMA procurement guidance is authoritative when public-funding rules apply, but private projects may use materially different contract, supplier, and approval controls. Runtime must retrieve funding and contract context before enforcing those overlays.
